intent: Check whether the candidate can describe the minimal pieces needed to serve HTTP requests with Node.js before using a framework.
primaryQuestion: How do you create a basic HTTP server in Node.js?

expectedSignals.label: Mentions importing the built-in http module, for example require('http') or import http from 'node:http'.
Describes using http.createServer with a request listener that receives req and res for every incoming request.
Explains that the handler should set a status code and optionally headers such as Content-Type before writing the response.
Mentions ending the response with res.end() or equivalent so the client is not left waiting.
Mentions calling server.listen(port, callback) to bind the server to a port and start accepting connections.
Can give a small route-like example using req.method and req.url, while recognizing that this becomes verbose as the API grows.

redFlags: Says Node.js cannot create an HTTP server without Express.
Creates a handler but forgets to call listen, so no server actually starts.
Writes response data but never ends the response.
Confuses client-side fetch code with server-side request handling.
Thinks createServer automatically gives Express-style routing or JSON parsing.

scoringHints.description: Shows the createServer plus listen shape, explains req/res lifecycle, and notes why routing manually becomes repetitive.
Can describe the minimal server but misses one detail such as headers, status, or manual routing.
Has seen Node servers but cannot assemble the request listener and response completion flow clearly.
Answer does not show basic server-side Node HTTP knowledge.

followUps.question: Can you sketch the smallest GET /health endpoint using only the http module?
followUps.purpose: Force the candidate to make the server lifecycle concrete.

followUps.question: What part of that code starts accepting network connections?
followUps.purpose: Check whether they understand server.listen rather than only the handler.

en.title: Basic Node HTTP server
en.displayQuestion: How do you create a basic HTTP server in Node.js?
en.displayIntent: Check whether the candidate understands the built-in Node HTTP server lifecycle.
guidance: Look for http.createServer, a req/res handler, and server.listen.
Probe whether they know the response must be ended.
Ask how manual method and URL checks become routing.
commonMistakes: Assuming Express is required.
Forgetting server.listen.
Not ending the response.

vi.title: HTTP server cơ bản trong Node
vi.displayQuestion: Bạn tạo một HTTP server cơ bản trong Node.js như thế nào?
vi.displayIntent: Kiểm tra ứng viên có hiểu vòng đời server HTTP built-in của Node hay không.
guidance: Tìm các ý http.createServer, handler req/res và server.listen.
Đào sâu việc response phải được kết thúc.
Hỏi cách tự kiểm tra method và URL dẫn tới nhu cầu routing.
commonMistakes: Tưởng phải có Express mới tạo được server.
Quên server.listen.
Không kết thúc response.

ja.title: Node の基本 HTTP server
ja.displayQuestion: Node.js で基本的な HTTP server を作るにはどうしますか。
ja.displayIntent: Node の built-in HTTP server の基本ライフサイクルを理解しているか確認する。
guidance: http.createServer、req/res handler、server.listen に触れているかを見る。
response を終了する必要がある点を確認する。
method と URL の手動判定が routing につながることを聞く。
commonMistakes: Express が必須だと思う。
server.listen を忘れる。
response を終了しない。

sourceReferences.label: Node.js docs: HTTP
sourceReferences.note: Used for http.createServer, request listener, response, and server.listen concepts.

intent: Evaluate whether the candidate understands the practical problems Express solves compared with writing all HTTP handling directly.
primaryQuestion: What problem does Express solve compared with the built-in http module?

expectedSignals.label: Explains that the built-in http module is low-level and requires manually checking methods, paths, headers, body streams, and response formatting.
Describes Express as a minimal web framework that adds routing, middleware, request/response helpers, and better structure for web APIs.
Mentions that Express is built on top of Node's HTTP request/response model rather than replacing HTTP itself.
Gives examples of conveniences such as app.get, app.post, routers, express.json, res.status, res.json, and centralized middleware.
Recognizes trade-offs: Express reduces boilerplate but developers still need to understand HTTP semantics, validation, errors, and security.

redFlags: Says Express makes Node.js asynchronous or is required for async code.
Claims Express is a separate web server unrelated to Node's HTTP model.
Believes Express automatically handles all security, validation, and database work.
Cannot name any concrete feature beyond saying it is easier.
Says the built-in http module cannot serve real requests.

scoringHints.description: Clearly contrasts low-level HTTP handling with Express routing, middleware, helpers, and app structure while preserving HTTP understanding.
Names routing and middleware but gives a shallow comparison of what manual HTTP handling requires.
Knows Express is common but cannot explain the abstraction it provides.
Answer contains incorrect claims about Express replacing HTTP or solving unrelated runtime problems.

followUps.question: What code would be more repetitive if you wrote the same API using only the http module?
followUps.purpose: Move from generic convenience to concrete framework responsibilities.

followUps.question: Does Express remove the need to know HTTP methods and status codes?
followUps.purpose: Check whether the candidate understands Express as an abstraction over HTTP, not a replacement.

en.title: Why Express exists
en.displayQuestion: What problem does Express solve compared with the built-in http module?
en.displayIntent: Check whether the candidate understands Express as a routing and middleware framework over Node HTTP.
guidance: Listen for routing, middleware, request parsing, and response helpers.
Confirm they know Express still uses HTTP concepts.
Ask for concrete boilerplate that Express removes.
commonMistakes: Saying Express makes Node asynchronous.
Thinking Express handles business logic automatically.
Forgetting HTTP still matters.

vi.title: Vì sao dùng Express
vi.displayQuestion: Express giải quyết vấn đề gì so với module http built-in?
vi.displayIntent: Kiểm tra ứng viên có hiểu Express là framework routing và middleware trên nền Node HTTP hay không.
guidance: Nghe các ý routing, middleware, parse request và helper response.
Xác nhận ứng viên vẫn hiểu các khái niệm HTTP.
Hỏi ví dụ boilerplate cụ thể mà Express loại bỏ.
commonMistakes: Nói Express làm Node bất đồng bộ.
Tưởng Express tự xử lý business logic.
Quên rằng HTTP vẫn quan trọng.

ja.title: Express の役割
ja.displayQuestion: built-in の http module と比べて Express はどんな問題を解決しますか。
ja.displayIntent: Express を Node HTTP 上の routing と middleware framework として理解しているか確認する。
guidance: routing、middleware、request parsing、response helper に触れるかを見る。
HTTP の知識がまだ必要だと理解しているか確認する。
Express が減らす boilerplate の具体例を聞く。
commonMistakes: Express が Node を非同期にすると言う。
Express が business logic を自動処理すると考える。
HTTP の理解が不要だと思う。

sourceReferences.label: Express docs: Hello world example
sourceReferences.note: Used for the minimal Express app shape and relationship to Node request and response objects.

sourceReferences.label: Express docs: Using middleware
sourceReferences.note: Used for Express as a routing and middleware web framework.

intent: Check whether the candidate can define Express routing as method plus path plus handler matching.

expectedSignals.label: Defines routing as deciding how the app responds to a request for a specific HTTP method and URL path.
Mentions the route shape app.METHOD(path, handler), such as app.get('/users/:id', handler).
Explains that method and path both matter, so GET /users and POST /users can execute different handlers.
Understands that route handlers receive req and res and should send a response or pass control.
Mentions route parameters, router modules, or route order as practical routing details.
Recognizes that if no route matches, the app should eventually return a 404 response.

redFlags: Defines routing as only naming URLs without mentioning handlers.
Confuses Express routing with frontend client-side routing.
Thinks a matching path is enough regardless of HTTP method.
Cannot provide a minimal app.get or router.get example.
Assumes Express automatically creates routes from database models.

scoringHints.description: Explains routing through method, path, handler, matching behavior, and response responsibility with a concrete example.
Defines routing and gives a route example but misses method matching or 404 behavior.
Recognizes app.get syntax but cannot explain what Express matches.
Answer does not show practical Express routing knowledge.

followUps.question: Can you write a GET /users/:id route and identify the method, path, and handler?
followUps.purpose: Force concrete understanding of route components.

followUps.question: If only GET /users exists, should POST /users hit that route?
followUps.purpose: Check method-aware route matching.

en.title: Express routing
en.displayIntent: Check whether the candidate understands routing as method and path mapped to a handler.
guidance: Ask for the method, path, and handler pieces.
Probe whether method matching is understood.
Look for response or next responsibility.
commonMistakes: Confusing backend routing with frontend navigation.
Ignoring HTTP method matching.
Forgetting what the handler must do.

vi.title: Routing trong Express
vi.displayIntent: Kiểm tra ứng viên có hiểu route là mapping giữa method, path và handler hay không.
guidance: Hỏi rõ các phần method, path và handler.
Đào sâu việc route match theo HTTP method.
Tìm hiểu trách nhiệm gửi response hoặc gọi next.
commonMistakes: Nhầm routing backend với điều hướng frontend.
Bỏ qua HTTP method.
Quên handler phải kết thúc request hoặc chuyển tiếp.

ja.title: Express routing
ja.displayQuestion: Express の routing とは何ですか。
ja.displayIntent: route を method と path から handler へ対応付けるものとして理解しているか確認する。
guidance: method、path、handler の要素を聞く。
HTTP method も match 条件になるか確認する。
response または next の責務を見る。
commonMistakes: backend routing と frontend navigation を混同する。
HTTP method の match を無視する。
handler が request を終える必要を忘れる。

sourceReferences.label: Express docs: Routing
sourceReferences.note: Used for route definition, method/path matching, and handler behavior.

intent: Evaluate whether the candidate can connect common Express route helpers to HTTP method semantics.
primaryQuestion: What is the difference between app.get, app.post, app.put, and app.delete?

expectedSignals.label: Explains that each helper registers a route handler for a specific HTTP method.
Connects app.get to reading or retrieving a representation without intended server-side mutation.
Connects app.post to creating resources, submitting data, or triggering operations that may not be idempotent.
Connects app.put to replacing or updating a resource at a known URI, usually with idempotent intent.
Connects app.delete to removing or deactivating a resource.
Notes that Express routes by method but does not enforce REST semantics; handler code and API design must do that.

redFlags: Says all methods are interchangeable because they can all return JSON.
Uses GET for destructive operations without recognizing the problem.
Believes app.post automatically inserts into a database.
Cannot explain the basic difference between reading, creating, updating, and deleting.
Thinks Express prevents developers from mutating data inside GET handlers.

scoringHints.description: Correctly maps route helpers to HTTP semantics and explains Express only performs method-specific routing.
Understands the common CRUD mapping but is shallow on idempotency or framework enforcement.
Can name the helpers but treats them mostly as style choices.
Answer is incorrect about HTTP method purpose or Express routing behavior.

followUps.question: Why is GET /users/:id/delete a risky design?
followUps.purpose: Probe safe method semantics and unintended side effects.

followUps.question: For a todo API, which method would you use to list todos, create one, replace one, and delete one?
followUps.purpose: Check applied method selection.

en.title: Express HTTP methods
en.displayQuestion: What is the difference between app.get, app.post, app.put, and app.delete?
en.displayIntent: Check whether the candidate maps Express helpers to HTTP method semantics.
guidance: Map each helper to the underlying HTTP verb.
Listen for read, create, update, and delete intent.
Confirm Express does not enforce business semantics.
commonMistakes: Treating all methods as interchangeable.
Using GET for mutations.
Assuming Express performs database actions automatically.

vi.title: HTTP methods trong Express
vi.displayQuestion: app.get, app.post, app.put và app.delete khác nhau như thế nào?
vi.displayIntent: Kiểm tra ứng viên có map helper của Express với ý nghĩa HTTP method hay không.
guidance: Map từng helper với HTTP verb tương ứng.
Nghe các ý đọc, tạo, cập nhật và xóa.
Xác nhận Express không tự enforce semantic nghiệp vụ.
commonMistakes: Xem mọi method là thay thế được cho nhau.
Dùng GET cho thao tác thay đổi dữ liệu.
Tưởng Express tự thao tác database.

ja.title: Express の HTTP methods
ja.displayQuestion: app.get、app.post、app.put、app.delete の違いは何ですか。
ja.displayIntent: Express helper を HTTP method の意味に結び付けて説明できるか確認する。
guidance: 各 helper を対応する HTTP verb に mapping する。
read、create、update、delete の意図を聞く。
Express が業務 semantic を自動 enforce しない点を確認する。
commonMistakes: すべての method を交換可能だと考える。
mutation に GET を使う。
Express が database 操作を自動で行うと思う。

sourceReferences.label: Express docs: Route methods
sourceReferences.note: Used for Express method-based route helpers.

sourceReferences.label: MDN: HTTP request methods
sourceReferences.note: Used for common HTTP method semantics.

intent: Check whether the candidate understands the request and response objects used inside Express route handlers.
primaryQuestion: What are req and res in an Express route handler?

expectedSignals.label: Explains that req represents the incoming HTTP request and contains data such as method, URL, headers, params, query, body, cookies, and authentication context if middleware adds it.
Explains that res represents the HTTP response being built and is used to set status, headers, cookies, and send data.
Mentions common response helpers such as res.status(), res.json(), res.send(), res.redirect(), or res.end().
Understands that req and res are per-request objects and should not be stored globally for later reuse.
Mentions that a handler should normally send one final response or delegate control, not send multiple responses.
Recognizes that Express extends or wraps Node's request and response objects with convenience methods.

redFlags: Thinks req is a database request and res is the returned data object.
Cannot name where to read path parameters, query string, headers, or body.
Sends multiple responses from the same request path without recognizing headers-sent errors.
Stores req or res in global state for background work.
Believes returning a JavaScript object from the handler automatically sends JSON in Express.

scoringHints.description: Clearly explains req and res, common fields/helpers, per-request lifecycle, and one-response responsibility.
Defines req and res correctly but misses lifecycle or duplicate response nuance.
Knows the names but cannot use them to describe practical request handling.
Answer confuses Express handler objects with unrelated application data.

followUps.question: Where would you read an Authorization header, a URL id, and a JSON payload?
followUps.purpose: Check practical req usage across common request data sources.

followUps.question: What happens if one branch calls res.json() and later code calls res.send() again?
followUps.purpose: Probe response lifecycle and duplicate send awareness.

en.title: req and res
en.displayQuestion: What are req and res in an Express route handler?
en.displayIntent: Check whether the candidate understands request data access and response construction.
guidance: Ask what lives on req and what res is used for.
Probe common helpers like res.status and res.json.
Check that they avoid multiple responses.
commonMistakes: Thinking returning an object sends JSON automatically.
Mixing up request and response headers.
Sending more than one final response.

vi.title: req và res
vi.displayQuestion: req và res trong Express route handler là gì?
vi.displayIntent: Kiểm tra ứng viên có hiểu cách đọc request và tạo response hay không.
guidance: Hỏi req chứa gì và res dùng để làm gì.
Đào sâu các helper như res.status và res.json.
Kiểm tra việc tránh gửi nhiều response.
commonMistakes: Tưởng return object là tự gửi JSON.
Nhầm request header với response header.
Gửi nhiều response cuối cùng.

ja.title: req と res
ja.displayQuestion: Express route handler の req と res は何ですか。
ja.displayIntent: request data の取得と response の構築を理解しているか確認する。
guidance: req に何が入り、res を何に使うか聞く。
res.status や res.json などの helper を確認する。
複数 response を避ける理解を見る。
commonMistakes: object を return すれば自動で JSON になると思う。
request header と response header を混同する。
最終 response を複数送る。

sourceReferences.label: Express docs: Hello world example
sourceReferences.note: Used for basic req and res route handler usage.

sourceReferences.label: Express 5.x API
sourceReferences.note: Used for Express request and response object helpers.

intent: Verify whether the candidate understands Express middleware as request pipeline functions, not just route handlers.

expectedSignals.label: Defines middleware as a function in the request-response cycle that has access to req, res, and next.
Explains that middleware can run before route handlers, after earlier middleware, or at route/router/application level depending on registration.
Mentions common uses such as logging, authentication, authorization, parsing JSON, validation, CORS, rate limiting, and error handling.
Understands middleware can modify req or res, end the response, or call next to continue.
Mentions that registration order matters because Express runs matching middleware in sequence.
Can distinguish normal middleware from error-handling middleware at a high level.

redFlags: Says middleware is only authentication code.
Thinks middleware always runs after route handlers.
Does not know the role of next.
Assumes middleware must always send a response.
Ignores registration order and matching paths.

scoringHints.description: Explains middleware as ordered pipeline functions with req/res/next, common use cases, and continue-versus-end behavior.
Defines middleware and gives examples but misses order, path matching, or response lifecycle.
Has used middleware names but cannot explain the pipeline model.
Answer does not demonstrate practical Express middleware knowledge.

followUps.question: Where would you place an authentication middleware relative to protected routes?
followUps.purpose: Check order and route scoping.

followUps.question: What are two examples of middleware that do not directly belong to one business route?
followUps.purpose: Probe cross-cutting concern understanding.

en.title: Express middleware
en.displayIntent: Check whether the candidate understands middleware as ordered request pipeline functions.
guidance: Look for req, res, and next.
Ask what middleware can do to the request or response.
Probe registration order and common use cases.
commonMistakes: Reducing middleware to authentication only.
Ignoring order.
Forgetting next or response completion.

vi.title: Middleware trong Express
vi.displayIntent: Kiểm tra ứng viên có hiểu middleware là các hàm trong pipeline request hay không.
guidance: Tìm các ý req, res và next.
Hỏi middleware có thể làm gì với request hoặc response.
Đào sâu thứ tự đăng ký và use case phổ biến.
commonMistakes: Thu hẹp middleware thành authentication.
Bỏ qua thứ tự chạy.
Quên next hoặc việc kết thúc response.

ja.title: Express middleware
ja.displayQuestion: Express middleware とは何ですか。
ja.displayIntent: middleware を順序付きの request pipeline function として理解しているか確認する。
guidance: req、res、next に触れるかを見る。
middleware が request や response に何をできるか聞く。
登録順と一般的な use case を確認する。
commonMistakes: middleware を authentication だけに限定する。
実行順を無視する。
next や response completion を忘れる。

sourceReferences.label: Express docs: Using middleware
sourceReferences.note: Used for middleware definition, capabilities, and next behavior.

intent: Check whether the candidate understands next() as explicit control transfer in the Express middleware chain.
primaryQuestion: What does next() do in middleware?

expectedSignals.label: Explains that next() passes control to the next matching middleware or route handler in the Express stack.
Understands that middleware should call next() only when it has not ended the response and wants processing to continue.
Mentions next(err) as the way to forward an error to error-handling middleware.
Recognizes that calling next() more than once can cause confusing flow, duplicate handlers, or duplicate responses.
Mentions that code after next() may still run unless the function returns or control flow is structured carefully.
Can describe a practical example such as authentication middleware calling next only after a token is valid.

redFlags: Says next() sends the response to the client.
Thinks next() always routes to the next URL path instead of the next handler in the stack.
Calls next() after res.json() as a default habit without understanding duplicate response risk.
Does not know how errors are passed to centralized error middleware.
Assumes Express automatically calls next() if the middleware forgets.

scoringHints.description: Explains normal next, next(err), response-end boundaries, and exactly-once control flow with a practical example.
Understands next as passing control but misses error flow or duplicate-call hazards.
Knows next exists but cannot reason about when to call it.
Answer is incorrect about middleware control flow.

followUps.question: Should middleware call next() after it already sent a 401 response? Why or why not?
followUps.purpose: Check response lifecycle boundaries.

followUps.question: How would you pass a database error from middleware to a centralized error handler?
followUps.purpose: Probe next(err) awareness.

en.title: next in middleware
en.displayQuestion: What does next() do in middleware?
en.displayIntent: Check whether the candidate understands explicit control transfer in Express.
guidance: Listen for next matching middleware or handler.
Probe when not to call next.
Ask about next(err) for errors.
commonMistakes: Calling next after sending a response.
Thinking next sends the response.
Forgetting error forwarding.

vi.title: next trong middleware
vi.displayIntent: Kiểm tra ứng viên có hiểu việc chuyển quyền xử lý rõ ràng trong Express hay không.
guidance: Nghe ý chuyển sang middleware hoặc handler match tiếp theo.
Đào sâu khi nào không nên gọi next.
Hỏi về next(err) cho luồng lỗi.
commonMistakes: Gọi next sau khi đã gửi response.
Tưởng next gửi response.
Quên cách forward lỗi.

ja.title: middleware の next
ja.displayQuestion: middleware の next() は何をしますか。
ja.displayIntent: Express における明示的な control transfer を理解しているか確認する。
guidance: 次に match する middleware または handler に渡す説明を聞く。
next を呼ばない場面を確認する。
error では next(err) を使うか聞く。
commonMistakes: response 送信後に next を呼ぶ。
next が response を送ると思う。
error forwarding を忘れる。

sourceReferences.label: Express docs: Using middleware
sourceReferences.note: Used for next middleware function and request-response cycle behavior.

intent: Verify whether the candidate can reason about a hung request caused by middleware that neither ends the response nor passes control.
primaryQuestion: What happens if middleware does not call next() or send a response?

expectedSignals.label: Explains that the request-response cycle is not completed, so the client keeps waiting until a timeout or connection close.
States that later matching middleware and route handlers will not run because control was not passed onward.
Distinguishes this bug from an intentional middleware that sends a response, such as returning 401 for unauthorized access.
Mentions that the fix is to either send/end a response or call next/next(err) on every code path.
Recognizes common causes such as missing return in a conditional branch, swallowed async error, or forgot next inside validation/auth middleware.
Can describe debugging by checking logs, browser/network pending requests, and middleware order.

redFlags: Says Express automatically continues to the next route.
Says Express automatically sends a 404 if middleware stops without next.
Does not understand why the browser request remains pending.
Suggests calling next after every res.send without considering duplicate response risk.
Ignores unhandled async branches that never resolve the request.

scoringHints.description: Explains hanging requests, blocked later handlers, all-code-path completion, and practical debugging.
Knows the request hangs but gives limited detail on why or how to fix every branch.
Has seen pending requests but cannot connect them to middleware control flow.
Answer incorrectly assumes Express automatically recovers.

followUps.question: In an auth middleware, how would you structure valid and invalid token branches so every request completes?
followUps.purpose: Check practical branch control and response completion.

followUps.question: Why would placing a middleware that never calls next before all routes make the whole API look down?
followUps.purpose: Probe pipeline order and blocking behavior.

en.title: Middleware that hangs
en.displayQuestion: What happens if middleware does not call next() or send a response?
en.displayIntent: Check whether the candidate can diagnose a stalled Express request.
guidance: Look for request left hanging or pending.
Ask how later handlers are affected.
Probe every branch sending, ending, nexting, or next(err).
commonMistakes: Thinking Express automatically continues.
Thinking Express automatically sends 404.
Calling next after a final response without thought.

vi.title: Middleware làm request treo
vi.displayQuestion: Điều gì xảy ra nếu middleware không gọi next() và cũng không gửi response?
vi.displayIntent: Kiểm tra ứng viên có chẩn đoán được request Express bị pending hay không.
guidance: Tìm ý request bị treo hoặc pending.
Hỏi handler phía sau bị ảnh hưởng thế nào.
Đào sâu mọi nhánh phải send, end, next hoặc next(err).
commonMistakes: Tưởng Express tự chạy tiếp.
Tưởng Express tự trả 404.
Gọi next sau final response một cách máy móc.

ja.title: 止まる middleware
ja.displayQuestion: middleware が next() も response 送信もしない場合、何が起きますか。
ja.displayIntent: Express request が pending になる原因を診断できるか確認する。
guidance: request が hanging または pending になる説明を聞く。
後続 handler への影響を聞く。
すべての分岐で send、end、next、next(err) のいずれかが必要か確認する。
commonMistakes: Express が自動で続行すると思う。
Express が自動で 404 を返すと思う。
final response 後に機械的に next を呼ぶ。

sourceReferences.label: Express docs: Using middleware
sourceReferences.note: Used for the requirement to end the request-response cycle or call next.

intent: Check whether the candidate can choose the correct Express request data source for path identity, URL options, and payloads.
primaryQuestion: What is the difference between req.params, req.query, and req.body?

expectedSignals.label: Explains that req.params contains named path parameters declared in the route pattern, such as /users/:id.
Explains that req.query contains URL query string values after ?, often used for filters, pagination, sorting, search, and optional flags.
Explains that req.body contains parsed request payload data, commonly used for POST, PUT, and PATCH create/update operations.
Mentions that req.body depends on body-parsing middleware such as express.json or express.urlencoded registered before the route.
Treats all three as user-controlled input that requires validation, normalization, and type conversion.
Can give a concrete example such as GET /users/:id?include=posts using params and query, versus POST /users using body.

redFlags: Says params, query, and body are interchangeable.
Assumes req.query values are already numbers or booleans.
Forgets body parser middleware is required for JSON request bodies.
Uses request body for required resource identity without recognizing API design issues.
Trusts any of these values without validation.

scoringHints.description: Separates path identity, URL options, and payloads with examples, parser ordering, validation, and type conversion.
Defines the three correctly but misses parser middleware or untrusted input.
Can define one or two properties but cannot choose consistently in endpoint design.
Answer shows confusion about where Express stores request data.

followUps.question: For GET /products/:category?page=2 and POST /products, which values would you read from params, query, and body?
followUps.purpose: Force concrete mapping between URL shape and request properties.

followUps.question: Why might req.query.limit be a string even when the client sends ?limit=10?
followUps.purpose: Check type conversion and validation awareness.

en.title: params, query, and body
en.displayQuestion: What is the difference between req.params, req.query, and req.body?
en.displayIntent: Check whether the candidate can choose the right request data source.
guidance: Use params for path identity.
Use query for URL modifiers.
Use body for submitted payloads and mention parsing.
commonMistakes: Treating all three as interchangeable.
Forgetting express.json for body data.
Skipping validation and type conversion.

vi.title: params, query và body
vi.displayQuestion: req.params, req.query và req.body khác nhau như thế nào?
vi.displayIntent: Kiểm tra ứng viên có chọn đúng nguồn dữ liệu request hay không.
guidance: Dùng params cho định danh trong path.
Dùng query cho tùy chọn trên URL.
Dùng body cho payload gửi lên và nhắc tới parsing.
commonMistakes: Xem cả ba là thay thế được cho nhau.
Quên express.json cho body data.
Bỏ qua validation và chuyển kiểu.

ja.title: params、query、body
ja.displayQuestion: req.params、req.query、req.body の違いは何ですか。
ja.displayIntent: 適切な request data source を選べるか確認する。
guidance: path identity には params を使う。
URL modifiers には query を使う。
送信 payload には body を使い、parsing に触れる。
commonMistakes: 三つを交換可能だと扱う。
body data に express.json が必要なことを忘れる。
validation と type conversion を省く。

sourceReferences.label: Express docs: Route parameters
sourceReferences.note: Used for path parameter behavior.

sourceReferences.label: Express 5.x API: req.params, req.query, req.body
sourceReferences.note: Used for Express request data properties and parser requirements.

intent: Verify whether the candidate understands JSON body parsing middleware and its placement in the Express pipeline.

expectedSignals.label: Explains that express.json() is built-in middleware for parsing incoming JSON request bodies.
States that it populates req.body with the parsed JavaScript object when the request has a matching JSON Content-Type.
Mentions it must be registered before routes that need req.body.
Distinguishes express.json() from res.json(): one parses incoming request bodies, the other sends JSON responses.
Mentions handling invalid JSON and considering size limits or validation after parsing.
Recognizes that parsing does not make input trusted; schema validation and sanitization may still be needed.

redFlags: Says express.json() is used to return JSON responses.
Thinks it is needed for req.params or req.query.
Registers it after routes and expects earlier handlers to see req.body.
Assumes it parses every body type including multipart files.
Trusts parsed body values without validation.

scoringHints.description: Explains incoming JSON parsing, req.body population, middleware order, Content-Type, invalid JSON, and validation boundaries.
Knows it parses JSON body into req.body but misses order, Content-Type, or validation nuance.
Has used express.json but confuses it with response JSON helpers.
Answer is unsafe or incorrect about request body parsing.

followUps.question: What is the difference between app.use(express.json()) and res.json({ ok: true })?
followUps.purpose: Separate request parsing from response serialization.

followUps.question: What happens if the client sends invalid JSON to a route using express.json()?
followUps.purpose: Probe error handling and parser behavior.

en.title: express.json
en.displayIntent: Check whether the candidate understands JSON request body parsing.
guidance: Clarify it parses incoming JSON.
Ask about req.body and middleware order.
Separate express.json from res.json.
commonMistakes: Thinking it sends JSON responses.
Using it for query or params.
Skipping validation after parsing.

vi.title: express.json
vi.displayQuestion: Tại sao chúng ta dùng express.json()?
vi.displayIntent: Kiểm tra ứng viên có hiểu việc parse JSON request body hay không.
guidance: Làm rõ nó parse JSON đi vào.
Hỏi về req.body và thứ tự middleware.
Phân biệt express.json với res.json.
commonMistakes: Tưởng nó gửi JSON response.
Dùng nó cho query hoặc params.
Bỏ qua validation sau khi parse.

ja.title: express.json
ja.displayQuestion: なぜ express.json() を使いますか。
ja.displayIntent: JSON request body parsing を理解しているか確認する。
guidance: incoming JSON を parse するものだと確認する。
req.body と middleware order を聞く。
express.json と res.json を分ける。
commonMistakes: JSON response を送るためだと思う。
query や params のために使うと思う。
parse 後の validation を省く。

sourceReferences.label: Express 5.x API: express.json
sourceReferences.note: Used for JSON body parser behavior.

sourceReferences.label: Express 5.x API: req.body
sourceReferences.note: Used for request body population and untrusted input guidance.

intent: Check whether the candidate can describe basic RESTful API design beyond simply returning JSON.
primaryQuestion: What makes an API RESTful at a basic level?

expectedSignals.label: Explains that RESTful APIs model resources with stable URIs, such as /users and /users/:id, rather than only action names.
Connects standard HTTP methods to resource operations, such as GET to read, POST to create, PUT/PATCH to update, and DELETE to remove.
Mentions stateless requests: each request should carry the information needed to authenticate and process it without relying on conversational server state.
Mentions using representations such as JSON and appropriate Content-Type headers.
Mentions meaningful HTTP status codes and predictable error responses.
Recognizes that basic RESTfulness is not guaranteed just because an endpoint uses Express or returns JSON.

redFlags: Says REST means any API that returns JSON.
Uses only verb-based URLs such as /getUsers or /deleteUser without recognizing resource modeling issues.
Ignores HTTP methods and status codes.
Thinks REST requires no authentication or no database.
Treats every Express route as automatically RESTful.

scoringHints.description: Explains resources, URIs, method semantics, statelessness, representations, status codes, and the JSON-is-not-enough distinction.
Covers resources and methods but misses statelessness, representation, or status code details.
Describes common CRUD endpoints but cannot separate REST principles from framework habits.
Answer incorrectly equates REST with JSON or Express.

followUps.question: How would you design endpoints for listing users, fetching one user, creating a user, and deleting a user?
followUps.purpose: Move the answer into concrete resource and method design.

followUps.question: Why is POST /createUser less RESTful than POST /users?
followUps.purpose: Probe resource-oriented URI design.

en.title: Basic RESTfulness
en.displayQuestion: What makes an API RESTful at a basic level?
en.displayIntent: Check whether the candidate understands resource-oriented HTTP API design.
guidance: Look for resources and stable URIs.
Ask how HTTP methods map to operations.
Probe statelessness, representations, and status codes.
commonMistakes: Equating REST with JSON.
Using only action-style URLs.
Ignoring HTTP method semantics.

vi.title: RESTful cơ bản
vi.displayQuestion: Ở mức cơ bản, điều gì làm một API được xem là RESTful?
vi.displayIntent: Kiểm tra ứng viên có hiểu thiết kế HTTP API theo resource hay không.
guidance: Tìm các ý resource và URI ổn định.
Hỏi cách HTTP method map với thao tác.
Đào sâu statelessness, representation và status code.
commonMistakes: Đồng nhất REST với JSON.
Chỉ dùng URL kiểu action.
Bỏ qua ý nghĩa HTTP method.

ja.title: 基本的な RESTfulness
ja.displayQuestion: 基本レベルで API が RESTful であるとはどういうことですか。
ja.displayIntent: resource-oriented な HTTP API design を理解しているか確認する。
guidance: resource と安定した URI に触れるかを見る。
HTTP method が operation にどう対応するか聞く。
statelessness、representation、status code を確認する。
commonMistakes: REST を JSON と同一視する。
action-style URL だけを使う。
HTTP method semantics を無視する。

sourceReferences.label: Roy Fielding dissertation: REST architectural style
sourceReferences.note: Used for REST architectural framing and constraints.

sourceReferences.label: MDN: HTTP request methods
sourceReferences.note: Used for HTTP method semantics.

intent: Verify whether the candidate knows common HTTP status codes and can choose them for basic API outcomes.
primaryQuestion: Which HTTP status codes should a junior backend developer know?

expectedSignals.label: Names common success codes: 200 OK, 201 Created, and 204 No Content, with appropriate use cases.
Names common client error codes: 400 Bad Request, 401 Unauthorized, 403 Forbidden, 404 Not Found, and often 409 Conflict or 422 Unprocessable Content.
Names common server error code 500 Internal Server Error and understands it represents unexpected server failure.
Explains the broad classes: 2xx success, 3xx redirects, 4xx client/request problems, and 5xx server problems.
Can map practical cases: validation error, missing auth, authenticated but not allowed, missing resource, successful deletion, and created resource.
Recognizes that returning HTTP 200 for every outcome makes clients and observability worse.

redFlags: Returns 200 for errors and puts success false in the body as the only signal.
Uses 500 for validation errors or missing resources.
Confuses 401 authentication missing/invalid with 403 authenticated but forbidden.
Thinks status codes are only frontend concerns.
Cannot explain 201 versus 200 or 204 versus 200.

scoringHints.description: Knows common codes, their classes, and maps them accurately to real API outcomes.
Knows the main codes but is fuzzy on 401 versus 403, 201, or 204.
Can list codes but cannot choose them reliably for scenarios.
Answer shows unsafe or misleading status code usage.

followUps.question: What status would you return for invalid JSON, missing login token, no permission, and a deleted resource?
followUps.purpose: Check scenario-based code selection.

followUps.question: Why is always returning 200 with an error field in JSON a problem?
followUps.purpose: Probe client behavior, caches, monitoring, and HTTP semantics.

en.title: HTTP status codes
en.displayQuestion: Which HTTP status codes should a junior backend developer know?
en.displayIntent: Check whether the candidate can use common status codes correctly.
guidance: Ask for practical scenarios, not just memorized numbers.
Probe 401 versus 403 and 200 versus 201.
commonMistakes: Returning 200 for every outcome.
Using 500 for client mistakes.
Confusing 401 and 403.

vi.title: HTTP status codes
vi.displayQuestion: Junior backend developer nên biết những HTTP status code nào?
vi.displayIntent: Kiểm tra ứng viên có dùng đúng các status code phổ biến hay không.
guidance: Hỏi theo tình huống thực tế, không chỉ đọc số.
Đào sâu 401 với 403 và 200 với 201.
commonMistakes: Luôn trả 200 cho mọi kết quả.
Dùng 500 cho lỗi từ client.
Nhầm 401 và 403.

ja.title: HTTP status codes
ja.displayQuestion: Junior backend developer が知っておくべき HTTP status code は何ですか。
ja.displayIntent: 一般的な status code を正しく使えるか確認する。
guidance: 番号の暗記ではなく実例で確認する。
commonMistakes: すべての結果で 200 を返す。
client mistake に 500 を使う。
401 と 403 を混同する。

sourceReferences.label: MDN: HTTP response status codes
sourceReferences.note: Used for status code classes and common response codes.

intent: Check whether the candidate can return JSON correctly from an Express route while managing status and response lifecycle.
primaryQuestion: How do you return JSON from an Express route?

expectedSignals.label: Uses res.json(data) to serialize a JavaScript object or array and send a JSON response.
Mentions setting the status code with res.status(code).json(data), such as res.status(201).json(createdUser).
Understands that res.json ends the response for that request and later code should not send another response.
Mentions that Express sets the JSON Content-Type header appropriately.
Avoids returning sensitive fields such as passwords, tokens, or internal-only data in the response body.
Can distinguish returning JSON from parsing incoming JSON with express.json().

redFlags: Thinks return { ok: true } from the handler automatically sends JSON.
Uses JSON.stringify manually without understanding headers or response completion.
Calls res.json multiple times in the same request path.
Confuses res.json with express.json.
Returns raw database records containing sensitive fields without considering response shape.

scoringHints.description: Uses res.status().json(), explains response completion, Content-Type, response shaping, and distinction from request parsing.
Knows res.json but misses status chaining, completion, or sensitive field concerns.
Can send something but is unclear on JSON serialization and Express helpers.
Answer would not reliably send a correct JSON response.

followUps.question: How would you return a 201 response with a newly created user object?
followUps.purpose: Check status chaining and practical syntax.

followUps.question: What is the difference between res.json() and express.json()?
followUps.purpose: Separate response serialization from request body parsing.

en.title: Returning JSON
en.displayQuestion: How do you return JSON from an Express route?
en.displayIntent: Check whether the candidate can send JSON with correct status and lifecycle handling.
guidance: Look for res.json or res.status().json.
Check that one final response is sent.
Ask about sensitive fields and express.json confusion.
commonMistakes: Returning an object directly.
Sending multiple JSON responses.
Confusing res.json and express.json.

vi.title: Trả JSON
vi.displayQuestion: Bạn trả JSON từ Express route như thế nào?
vi.displayIntent: Kiểm tra ứng viên có gửi JSON đúng status và lifecycle hay không.
guidance: Tìm res.json hoặc res.status().json.
Kiểm tra chỉ gửi một final response.
Hỏi về dữ liệu nhạy cảm và nhầm lẫn với express.json.
commonMistakes: Return object trực tiếp.
Gửi nhiều JSON response.
Nhầm res.json với express.json.

ja.title: JSON response
ja.displayQuestion: Express route から JSON を返すにはどうしますか。
ja.displayIntent: 正しい status と lifecycle で JSON を送れるか確認する。
guidance: res.json または res.status().json を確認する。
final response が一つだけかを見る。
sensitive fields と express.json との混同を聞く。
commonMistakes: object を直接 return する。
JSON response を複数送る。
res.json と express.json を混同する。

sourceReferences.label: Express 5.x API: res.json
sourceReferences.note: Used for JSON response behavior.

sourceReferences.label: Express 5.x API: res.status
sourceReferences.note: Used for setting HTTP status before sending a response.

intent: Check whether the candidate knows how to return a proper 404 for unmatched Express routes.
primaryQuestion: How do you handle a route that does not exist?

expectedSignals.label: Explains that unmatched routes should return a 404 Not Found response rather than a 500 server error.
Mentions adding a catch-all 404 middleware or route after all normal routes have been registered.
Shows the basic shape app.use((req, res) => res.status(404).json({ error: 'Not found' })) or equivalent.
Understands route order matters: placing the catch-all before real routes would block them.
Mentions returning a consistent JSON error shape for APIs.
Can distinguish 404 handling from centralized error-handling middleware for thrown or passed errors.

redFlags: Returns 500 for unknown routes.
Registers the catch-all route before the actual routes.
Redirects every unknown API route to the frontend without considering API clients.
Leaves unmatched API requests hanging.
Says Express always returns the exact JSON 404 format the application needs without custom handling.

scoringHints.description: Describes after-routes catch-all 404 handling, correct status, JSON shape, order, and distinction from error middleware.
Knows to return 404 but misses order or error-middleware distinction.
Has seen 404s but cannot implement reliable Express fallback handling.
Answer would misclassify or mishandle unmatched routes.

followUps.question: Where in app.js would you place the 404 handler relative to normal routes and error middleware?
followUps.purpose: Probe Express order and pipeline understanding.

followUps.question: Why is an unknown route usually 404 instead of 500?
followUps.purpose: Check client versus server error classification.

en.title: Handling missing routes
en.displayQuestion: How do you handle a route that does not exist?
en.displayIntent: Check whether the candidate can implement API 404 handling in Express.
guidance: Look for a catch-all after normal routes.
Confirm 404, not 500.
Ask about JSON error shape and middleware order.
commonMistakes: Putting the 404 handler first.
Returning 500.
Leaving the request hanging.

vi.title: Xử lý route không tồn tại
vi.displayQuestion: Bạn xử lý một route không tồn tại như thế nào?
vi.displayIntent: Kiểm tra ứng viên có implement được 404 cho API Express hay không.
guidance: Tìm catch-all sau các route bình thường.
Xác nhận là 404, không phải 500.
Hỏi về JSON error shape và thứ tự middleware.
commonMistakes: Đặt 404 handler ở đầu.
Trả 500.
Để request bị treo.

ja.title: 存在しない route の処理
ja.displayQuestion: 存在しない route はどう扱いますか。
ja.displayIntent: Express API の 404 handling を実装できるか確認する。
guidance: 通常 route の後に catch-all があるかを見る。
500 ではなく 404 か確認する。
JSON error shape と middleware order を聞く。
commonMistakes: 404 handler を最初に置く。
500 を返す。
request を hanging にする。

sourceReferences.label: Express docs: Hello world example
sourceReferences.note: Used for default 404 behavior when no route matches.

sourceReferences.label: Express docs: Using middleware
sourceReferences.note: Used for middleware ordering and request-response cycle behavior.

intent: Evaluate whether the candidate understands why CORS appears in browser-to-backend calls and how to configure it safely at a basic level.
primaryQuestion: What is CORS, and why does it appear when frontend calls backend APIs?

expectedSignals.label: Defines CORS as Cross-Origin Resource Sharing, a browser security mechanism around cross-origin HTTP requests.
Explains that origins differ by scheme, host, or port, so http://localhost:3000 calling http://localhost:5000 is cross-origin.
Mentions that browsers enforce same-origin policy for frontend JavaScript and require the server to allow the origin with CORS response headers.
Mentions common headers or configuration such as Access-Control-Allow-Origin, allowed methods, allowed headers, and credentials handling.
Explains preflight OPTIONS requests for non-simple requests such as custom headers, JSON content type, or methods like PUT/DELETE.
Recognizes that CORS is enforced by browsers, not usually by curl or server-to-server requests, and that disabling it broadly can be unsafe.

redFlags: Says CORS is a backend bug unrelated to browser security.
Thinks CORS is authentication or authorization.
Suggests always using Access-Control-Allow-Origin: * together with credentials.
Tries to fix CORS only in frontend code while the server never sends allow headers.
Does not understand why different localhost ports count as different origins.
Ignores preflight OPTIONS when debugging failed frontend API calls.

scoringHints.description: Explains browser same-origin policy, origin components, server allow headers, preflight, credentials, and safe configuration.
Understands browser cross-origin blocking and server headers but misses preflight or credentials nuance.
Has seen CORS errors but explains them as generic network or frontend issues.
Answer recommends unsafe or incorrect CORS handling.

followUps.question: Why can curl call the API successfully while the browser shows a CORS error?
followUps.purpose: Check browser-enforced policy understanding.

followUps.question: What changes when the frontend sends cookies or Authorization headers?
followUps.purpose: Probe credentials, allowed headers, and preflight behavior.

en.title: CORS basics
en.displayQuestion: What is CORS, and why does it appear when frontend calls backend APIs?
en.displayIntent: Check whether the candidate understands browser cross-origin protection and server CORS headers.
guidance: Look for same-origin policy and origin definition.
Ask about server allow headers and preflight OPTIONS.
Probe safe handling of credentials and allowed origins.
commonMistakes: Treating CORS as authentication.
Trying to fix it only in frontend code.
Using wildcard origins unsafely with credentials.

vi.title: CORS cơ bản
vi.displayQuestion: CORS là gì, và tại sao nó xuất hiện khi frontend gọi backend API?
vi.displayIntent: Kiểm tra ứng viên có hiểu bảo vệ cross-origin của browser và CORS header từ server hay không.
guidance: Tìm same-origin policy và định nghĩa origin.
Hỏi về allow header từ server và preflight OPTIONS.
Đào sâu cách xử lý credentials và allowed origins an toàn.
commonMistakes: Xem CORS là authentication.
Chỉ cố sửa ở frontend.
Dùng wildcard origin không an toàn với credentials.

ja.title: CORS の基礎
ja.displayQuestion: CORS とは何で、frontend が backend API を呼ぶときになぜ出てきますか。
ja.displayIntent: browser の cross-origin protection と server の CORS header を理解しているか確認する。
guidance: same-origin policy と origin の定義を聞く。
server allow headers と preflight OPTIONS を確認する。
credentials と allowed origins の安全な扱いを深掘りする。
commonMistakes: CORS を authentication として扱う。
frontend だけで直そうとする。
credentials で wildcard origin を安全でない形で使う。

sourceReferences.label: MDN: Cross-Origin Resource Sharing
sourceReferences.note: Used for same-origin policy, CORS headers, preflight, and credentials behavior.
